claim: Route stability does not imply mediated-function stability.
basis: Controlled counterexample and route/latent/output drift diagnostics in the v0.7–v0.9 line.

claim: Context can be inferred and used for guarded routing in the RC1b overlap-chain setting.
basis: Validation-selected context posterior and guarded global head across five seeds.

claim: A mixed host ecology appears on some MNIST analyses.
basis: Dominance, contribution, and ablation signals are positive for only a subset of host-task pairs.

claim: Stronger geometry or memory constraints can improve stability while harming plasticity.
basis: Positive and negative ablations across multiple prototype branches.

claim: Coverage must be reported separately from competence and actionability.
basis: CAL five-axis decomposition and conformal calibration analyses.

claim: Goal-, cost-, memory-, and evidence-aware control can unify MMALS decisions.
basis: Conceptual design; full implementation and confirmatory evidence pending.
